  storefront_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monetization.storefront_item. Business justification: A player subscription is initiated through the purchase of a subscription product listed as a storefront_item. Linking player_subscription.storefront_item_id → storefront_item.storefront_item_id conne']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.subscription. Business justification: Subscription dunning, renewal billing, and payment failure handling require linking the monetization player_subscription record to the billing.subscription lifecycle record. Revenue operations teams r']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that defines the entitlements, pricing, and billing cycle for this subscription.']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.team. Business justification: Team fan subscription passes (e.g., team-branded premium memberships) are a real esports monetization product. Linking player_subscription to team enables fan monetization reporting, team-level subscr']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The marketing channel through which the player was acquired and subsequently subscribed. Used for CPI (Cost Per Install) and ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|paid_social|paid_search|email|in_game_promotion|referral|influencer|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription will automatically renew at the end of the current billing period. True means the subscription will renew unless cancelled. False means the subscription will expire at period end.']
  billing_cycle_count varchar [note: 'The number of billing cycles that have been completed for this subscription. Used for retention cohort analysis and subscription age segmentation.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the subscription is billed. Determines billing cycle length, revenue recognition schedule, and churn calculation windows.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'The date when the player cancelled the subscription. Null if the subscription has not been cancelled. Used for churn analysis and retention campaigns.']
  cancellation_feedback varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback provided by the player at the time of cancellation. Provides qualitative insights into churn drivers and player sentiment.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided by the player for cancelling the subscription. Used for churn analysis, product improvement, and retention strategy development.']
  coppa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription complies with COPPA requirements for players under 13 years of age. Used to enforce parental consent and data collection restrictions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subscription record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the subscription price. Used for multi-currency reporting and revenue normalization.']
  current_billing_period_end date [note: 'The end date of the current billing cycle. The next renewal or cancellation will occur on or before this date.']
  current_billing_period_start date [note: 'The start date of the current billing cycle. Used to calculate prorated charges, usage windows, and renewal timing.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied to the subscription price. Null if no discount was applied. Used for net revenue calculation and promotion cost analysis.']
  discount_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a promotional discount or coupon was applied to this subscription. Used for promotion effectiveness analysis and revenue attribution.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'The promotional code or coupon code applied to the subscription. Null if no discount was applied. Used for campaign attribution and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the subscription price. Null if no discount was applied or if discount was a fixed amount. Used for promotion effectiveness reporting.']
  entitlement_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player currently has access to the subscription entitlements. May be false even if subscription status is active, due to payment failures or grace period expiration.']
  gdpr_consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR requirements. Required for players in EU jurisdictions.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'The date when the grace period for payment retry ends. After this date, the subscription will be suspended or cancelled if payment is not successful. Null if no grace period is active.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subscription record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing.']
  last_payment_failure_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent payment failure. Null if no payment failures have occurred. Used for dunning workflow triggers and grace period calculation.']
  lifetime_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'The total revenue generated from this subscription since inception, including all billing cycles. Used for LTV (Lifetime Value) calculation and whale segmentation.']
  next_billing_date date [note: 'The date when the next subscription charge will be processed. Null if subscription is cancelled or not set to auto-renew.']
  payment_failure_count varchar [note: 'The number of times payment processing has failed for this subscription. Used for involuntary churn risk scoring and dunning campaign targeting.']
  payment_method_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized reference to the payment method used for recurring billing. The token is provided by the payment processor and does not contain actual card numbers, ensuring PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) compliance.']
  payment_processor varchar [note: 'The payment processor or platform storefront that handles billing for this subscription. Determines reconciliation process, fee structure, and refund policies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stripe|paypal|apple_iap|google_play|steam|epic_games|console_first_party — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'The referral code used by the player when subscribing, if applicable. Links to the referring player for K-Factor (Viral Coefficient) calculation and referral reward distribution.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription is scheduled to end or did end. Null for active subscriptions with auto-renewal enabled. Populated for cancelled or expired subscriptions.']
  subscription_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The recurring price charged for the subscription per billing cycle. Stored in the currency specified in the currency_code field. Used for ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) and ARPPU (Average Revenue Per Paying User) calculations.']
  subscription_source varchar [note: 'Indicates how this subscription originated. New player indicates first subscription at account creation. Existing player upgrade indicates conversion from free-to-play. Reactivation indicates return of a previously churned subscriber. Cross-sell indicates purchase of an additional subscription product.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription became active and entitlements were first granted to the player. Used to calculate subscription age and LTV (Lifetime Value).']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription. Active indicates the subscription is in good standing and entitlements are available. Trial indicates the player is in a trial period. Paused indicates temporary suspension by player request. Cancelled indicates the player has terminated the subscription. Expired indicates the subscription ended due to non-renewal. Suspended indicates administrative hold due to payment failure or policy violation.']
  subscription_tier varchar [note: 'The tier or level of the subscription plan, indicating the scope of entitlements and benefits. Used for segmentation and upsell targeting.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'The date when the trial period ended and the subscription converted to paid status. Null if no trial was used or trial is still active.']
  trial_start_date date [note: 'The date when the trial period began. Null if no trial was used for this subscription.']
  trial_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the player has used a trial period for this subscription plan. Used to enforce one-trial-per-player business rules and prevent trial abuse.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a player\'s active or historical subscription to a subscription plan. Tracks player reference, plan reference, subscription start/end dates, current billing period, renewal status, cancellation reason, payment method token, platform storefront, trial used flag, and churn timestamp. The SSOT for individual player subscription lifecycle — feeds churn rate and LTV calculations.'
}

The entitlement must reference the cert that authorized the sale in t']

  purchase_price_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount paid by the player for this DLC if acquired through purchase. Null for non-purchase acquisitions.']

  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for why the entitlement was revoked.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a player\'s ownership of a specific DLC (Downloadable Content) or expansion pack. Captures player reference, DLC SKU reference, acquisition method (purchased, gifted, promotional, bundle), acquisition timestamp, platform storefront, license key token, download status, and revocation flag. The SSOT for DLC ownership rights — distinct from the MTX catalog definition.'
}

Table monetization.player_ltv_segment {
  player_ltv_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the player LTV segment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: GDPR profiling consent: LTV segmentation involves automated profiling of player spending behavior. Under GDPR Art. 22, the processing must reference the consent_policy that authorized this profiling a']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.league. Business justification: Esports league viewership is a proven LTV driver. Segmenting player LTV by league affiliation enables targeted monetization campaigns, league-specific ARPU reporting for sponsor valuation, and identif']
  ml_model_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ml_model_registry. Business justification: ML model lineage for LTV scoring: LTV segments are computed by ML models; the segment record must reference the model that produced it for model governance, auditability, and retraining decisions. Gam']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player being segmented. Links to the player master record in the Player domain.']
  player_behavior_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_behavior_segment. Business justification: LTV-to-behavior segment reconciliation: gaming studios cross-reference monetization LTV segments with analytics behavior segments in unified player intelligence dashboards. This join is essential for ']
  player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.player_segment. Business justification: LTV segments (whale, dolphin, minnow) are monetization-derived classifications that feed marketing segmentation for targeting, personalization, and lookalike modeling. Essential for audience building,']
  primary_game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title where the player has generated the most revenue. Used for cross-promotion and portfolio analysis.']
  processing_activity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.processing_activity. Business justification: GDPR Records of Processing Activities (RoPA): LTV segmentation is a documented processing activity under GDPR Article 30. The segment record must reference the processing_activity that documents this ']
  spend_limit_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.spend_limit_control. Business justification: Responsible gambling monitoring: LTV segmentation is used to identify high-spend players approaching regulatory spend limits. The segment record references the applicable spend_limit_control to enable']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform (mobile, console, PC) where the player has generated the most revenue. Used for platform-specific monetization strategies.']
  arppu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average revenue per paying user over the measurement period (typically 30 days), in USD. Calculated only for players who made at least one IAP transaction.']
  arpu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average revenue per user over the measurement period (typically 30 days), in USD. Includes both IAP and ad revenue divided by active days.']
  average_transaction_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average value per IAP transaction in USD. Calculated as total_iap_spend divided by total_transaction_count. Used to identify whale spending patterns.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Machine learning predicted probability of player churn in the next 30 days, ranging from 0.0000 (no risk) to 1.0000 (certain churn). Used for retention campaign targeting.']
  combined_ltv_score decimal(15,2) [note: 'Hybrid LTV score combining IAP spend and ad revenue to represent total player value in USD. Calculated as total_iap_spend + total_ad_revenue. Used for unified player value ranking.']
  conversion_probability_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Machine learning predicted probability that a F2P player will make their first purchase in the next 30 days, ranging from 0.0000 (no chance) to 1.0000 (certain conversion). Null for players who have already made a purchase.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the players primary transaction currency. All monetary amounts in this record are converted to USD, but this field preserves the players native currency for localization.']
  days_in_current_segment varchar [note: 'Number of days the player has been in the current LTV segment. Calculated as current date minus segment_assignment_date. Used for segment stability analysis.']
  days_since_last_purchase varchar [note: 'Number of days since the players last IAP transaction. Calculated as current date minus last_purchase_date. Null for F2P players. Used for churn prediction and reactivation campaigns.']
  first_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the players first IAP transaction. Null for F2P players who have never made a purchase. Used to calculate payer tenure and conversion funnel metrics.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the player based on their primary platform account location. NAM = North America, EUR = Europe, APAC = Asia-Pacific, LATAM = Latin America, MEA = Middle East and Africa. Used for regional monetization strategy and pricing localization.']
  is_dap boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the player is a daily active payer (made at least one IAP transaction in the last 24 hours). True = DAP, False = not DAP.']
  largest_single_transaction decimal(10,2) [note: 'The highest single IAP transaction amount the player has ever made, in USD. Used to identify whale potential and spending ceiling.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the players most recent IAP transaction. Null for F2P players. Used to identify lapsed payers and calculate recency metrics.']
  last_recalculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the LTV segment and associated metrics were last recalculated. Used to ensure data freshness and trigger scheduled updates.']
  predicted_ltv_30d decimal(10,2) [note: 'Machine learning predicted LTV for the next 30 days, in USD. Used for player acquisition ROI optimization and marketing budget allocation.']
  predicted_ltv_90d decimal(10,2) [note: 'Machine learning predicted LTV for the next 90 days, in USD. Used for cohort analysis and long-term player value forecasting.']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment method the player uses most frequently for IAP transactions. Used for payment optimization and fraud detection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay|carrier_billing|gift_card|cryptocurrency — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  previous_segment_label varchar [note: 'The LTV segment label the player held immediately before the current assignment. Used to track player progression and churn patterns. New Player indicates first-time segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whale|dolphin|minnow|f2p|at_risk_whale|lapsed_payer|new_player — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  segment_assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the player was assigned to the current LTV segment. Used to track segment tenure and transition history.']
  segment_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent segment transition. Spend Increase = player moved up tier, Spend Decrease = player moved down tier, Inactivity = player became inactive, Reactivation = lapsed player returned, First Purchase = F2P converted to payer, Churn = player stopped spending, Periodic Refresh = scheduled recalculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spend_increase|spend_decrease|inactivity|reactivation|first_purchase|churn|periodic_refresh — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the segment record. Active = player is actively segmented and metrics are current, Inactive = player has not been active recently, Suspended = player account is suspended, Archived = historical record for churned player.']
  total_ad_impressions varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of ad impressions served to the player across all games. Used to calculate ad engagement and eCPM metrics.']
  total_ad_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative lifetime ad revenue generated by the player through ad impressions, rewarded video views, and interstitial ads, in USD. Critical for F2P monetization models.']
  total_iap_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative lifetime spend on in-app purchases (IAP) by the player across all games and platforms, in USD. Includes microtransactions, DLC, battle passes, and subscription fees.']
  total_rewarded_ad_views varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of rewarded video ads the player has watched to completion. Indicates player willingness to engage with ads for in-game rewards.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of IAP transactions the player has completed across all games and platforms. Used to distinguish high-frequency low-value spenders from low-frequency high-value spenders.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master segmentation record classifying each player into a monetization value tier based on cumulative spend and ad engagement. Captures player reference, LTV segment label (F2P, minnow, dolphin, whale), total lifetime IAP spend, total ad revenue generated, combined LTV score, ARPU/ARPPU period metrics, DAP flag, segment assignment date, previous segment, and change reason. The SSOT for whale/dolphin/minnow classification — incorporates hybrid LTV (IAP + ad revenue) for mobile-first titles.'
}

Table monetization.ad_placement {
  ad_placement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ad placement definition. Primary key.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Ad placement A/B testing: placements are A/B tested on format, frequency caps, and location to optimize eCPM and fill rates. The existing ab_test_variant column is a denormalized variant label; a prop']
  ad_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_network. Business justification: Ad placements are configured per ad network for mediation waterfall setup. The existing `ad_network_partner` plain-text field is a denormalized reference to ad_network. Gaming ad monetization teams ne']
  ad_unit_id varchar [note: 'External ad network-specific unit identifier used to integrate this placement with the ad network SDK.']
  age_rating_cert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.age_rating_cert. Business justification: Age-gated ad serving: ad placements have age_gate_minimum and coppa_compliant flags. The placement must reference the age_rating_cert that governs permissible ad content and audience targeting rules. ']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: GDPR/COPPA ad consent governance: ad_placement has gdpr_consent_required as a plain flag — a denormalization of the consent_policy. The placement must reference the consent_policy that defines what co']
  dev_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.dev_project. Business justification: Ad placements are designed UX elements implemented by dev teams. Linking supports A/B testing attribution (which project version introduced the placement), ad-to-content performance analysis, and cros']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.community_event. Business justification: Ad placements scoped to specific community events (event-exclusive ad slots, sponsor integrations during tournaments/events) require this FK for event-specific ad targeting configuration and sponsor r']
  fallback_ad_placement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_placement (fallback_ad_placement_id)']
  game_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.game_mode. Business justification: Ad placements trigger in specific game modes (e.g., rewarded video after multiplayer match). Business optimizes ad yield and player experience by mode. Critical for ad ops, fill rate analysis, and UX ']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title where this ad placement is available.']
  league_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.league. Business justification: Ad inventory is managed at the league level in esports broadcast operations. League-scoped ad placements define the inventory available to sponsors and ad networks for a specific league, enabling leag']
  level_map_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.level_map. Business justification: In-game advertising (billboards, banners) is placed within specific level maps. Ad revenue attribution by map, fill rate analysis per level, and advertiser reporting on placement context all require t']
  licensed_ip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.licensed_ip. Business justification: Ad placements featuring licensed IP content (branded in-game placements using licensed franchises or characters) must reference the licensed IP for royalty tracking and compliance. Gaming companies wi']
  name_localization_string_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.localization_string. Business justification: Ad placement UI text (reward descriptions, call-to-action) needs localization for international audiences. Essential for global ad monetization and player comprehension.']
  player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.player_segment. Business justification: Ad ops teams configure which player segments are eligible for each ad placement (e.g., exclude paying players from rewarded ads, target churned users). The existing `player_segment_eligibility` plain-']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_policy. Business justification: Ad placements must comply with policies governing COPPA compliance, GDPR consent for behavioral targeting, and advertising restrictions to minors. Essential for placement approval process and regulato']
  reward_asset_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset_bundle. Business justification: Rewarded ads grant asset bundles as rewards (e.g., \'watch ad, receive starter pack\'). The existing reward_asset_id covers single-asset rewards, but bundle rewards require a separate FK. Ad monetizatio']
  reward_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.asset. Business justification: Rewarded video ads grant cosmetic assets (skins, emotes) as player incentives. Links ad placement to specific reward content for entitlement delivery and ad performance tracking.']
  sdk_integration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.sdk_integration. Business justification: Ad placements are served through specific SDK integrations (ad mediation SDKs) on each platform. Ad monetization operations require knowing which SDK integration serves each placement for fill rate tr']
  seasonal_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.seasonal_event. Business justification: Event-specific ad placements with themed rewards (holiday currency, event items). Links ad strategy to event lifecycle for targeted monetization and player re-engagement.']
  sponsorship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.sponsorship. Business justification: Sponsor-integrated ad placements (branded overlays, sponsor bumpers) are contractually defined in sponsorship agreements. Linking ad_placement to sponsorship enables delivery tracking against contract']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform where this ad placement is served (iOS, Android, Web).']
  tournament_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.tournament. Business justification: Tournament-specific ad placements (e.g., grand final broadcast ad units) are a distinct inventory category with premium eCPM floors. Linking ad_placement to tournament enables tournament-level ad reve']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Type of ad format served in this placement (rewarded video, interstitial, banner, native, playable, offerwall).']
  age_gate_minimum varchar [note: 'Minimum age required for a player to be eligible to see this ad placement, enforced for COPPA and regional compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad placement was approved for production deployment.']
  average_ctr_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average click-through rate percentage for this ad placement calculated from lifetime impressions and clicks.']
  average_ecpm_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average eCPM in USD achieved by this ad placement over its lifetime.']
  cooldown_seconds varchar [note: 'Minimum time in seconds that must elapse between consecutive displays of this ad placement to the same player.']
  coppa_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this ad placement is compliant with COPPA regulations for child-directed content.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad placement record was first created in the system.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this ad placement when multiple placements are eligible at the same trigger context (lower number = higher priority).']
  ecpm_floor_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum eCPM floor price in USD that ad networks must meet to serve ads in this placement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this ad placement is scheduled to be retired or deactivated (nullable for indefinite placements).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this ad placement becomes active and eligible to serve ads.']
  exclude_paying_players boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether players who have made any in-app purchase should be excluded from seeing this ad placement.']
  fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fill rate percentage for this ad placement representing the desired proportion of ad requests that should be filled with ads.']
  frequency_cap_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this ad placement can be shown to a player within a 24-hour period.']
  frequency_cap_per_session varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this ad placement can be shown to a player within a single game session.']
  geographic_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this ad placement is available (e.g., USA,CAN,GBR).']
  lifetime_clicks varchar [note: 'Total cumulative number of ad clicks recorded for this placement since activation.']
  lifetime_impressions varchar [note: 'Total cumulative number of ad impressions served through this placement since activation.']
  lifetime_revenue_usd decimal(15,4) [note: 'Total cumulative ad revenue in USD generated by this placement since activation.']
  mediation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ad mediation is enabled for this placement to optimize fill rate and eCPM across multiple networks.']
  minimum_player_level varchar [note: 'Minimum player level required to be eligible to see this ad placement.']
  placement_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the ad placement used across ad networks and internal systems.']
  placement_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments about this ad placement for operational reference and context.']
  placement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad placement indicating whether it is actively serving ads.']
  reward_amount varchar [note: 'Quantity of the reward granted to the player for completing the ad interaction (e.g., 100 coins, 1 life).']
  reward_currency_code varchar [note: 'Code of the virtual currency awarded as a reward for this ad placement (if reward_type is virtual_currency).']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-game reward granted to the player for engaging with this ad placement (applicable for rewarded ad formats).']
  trigger_context varchar [note: 'Game event or player action that triggers the ad placement (e.g., between_levels, opt_in_reward, storefront_entry, session_start, game_over).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad placement record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Master definition of in-game advertising placements available for monetization (rewarded video ads, interstitial ads, banner ads, native ads, playable ads). Captures placement name, ad format, game title scope, trigger context (between levels, opt-in reward, storefront), ad network partner (Unity Ads, ironSource, AdMob, AppLovin), fill rate target, eCPM floor, frequency cap, player segment eligibility, and active status. The SSOT for ad inventory definitions enabling hybrid F2P monetization (IAP + ads).'
}

Table monetization.ad_impression {
  ad_impression_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ad impression event. Primary key for the ad impression transactional record.']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment. Business justification: Ad placement location, format, frequency caps, and reward amounts are commonly A/B tested. Experiment attribution enables measurement of ad revenue impact, player retention effects, and optimal ad loa']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the specific ad creative that was displayed. Used for creative performance analysis and A/B testing.']
  ad_placement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ad placement configuration that defines where and how the ad was displayed within the game. Links to the ad placement master entity.']
  ad_unit_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for the ad unit within the ad network. Used for tracking and reconciliation with ad network reporting.']
  build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studio.build. Business justification: Ad impressions occur within specific builds. Tracking build version supports ad network certification (revenue reconciliation by certified build), A/B test attribution for ad placement changes, and fr']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'The advertising campaign identifier from the ad network. Used for campaign-level performance tracking and attribution.']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.consent_policy. Business justification: GDPR ad impression audit trail: each ad impression must record the consent_policy under which it was served. Data protection authorities require proof that impressions were served only to players who ']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to player.device. Business justification: Ad fraud detection and per-device frequency capping require linking impressions to the registered device record. Ad ops teams run device-level impression reports to identify emulator fraud and enforce']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to community.community_event. Business justification: Ad impressions served during community events require event attribution for sponsor campaign performance reporting and event monetization analytics. Gaming ad ops teams report eCPM and fill rates per ']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title in which the ad impression occurred. Links to the game title master entity.']
  match_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.match. Business justification: Ads served during match viewing (in-client spectator mode, companion apps). Core esports monetization model. Tracks ad revenue attribution to specific matches for broadcast rights valuation and sponso']
  network_region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to infrastructure.network_region. Business justification: Geographic ad targeting, regional eCPM analysis, GDPR/COPPA compliance by jurisdiction, and ad network partner selection by region require linking impressions to network regions for regulatory and rev']
  player_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player who viewed the ad impression. Links to the player master entity.']
  player_behavior_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.player_behavior_segment. Business justification: Segment-level ad revenue reporting: ad impressions are served to players based on behavior segments; attributing impressions to the formal segment definition enables segment-level eCPM and fill rate a']
  player_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.player_segment. Business justification: Ad impression records carry `player_segment` as a plain-text denormalized field. Normalizing to a FK enables segment-level ad revenue reporting (eCPM by segment, rewarded ad completion by LTV tier) — ']
  previous_ad_impression_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_impression (previous_ad_impression_id)']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the player session during which the ad impression occurred. Links to the session event entity.']
  sponsorship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to esports.sponsorship. Business justification: Sponsor impression delivery reporting is a contractual obligation in esports sponsorship deals. Linking ad_impression to sponsorship enables real-time tracking of contracted impression guarantees, per']
  storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the platform on which the ad impression occurred (iOS, Android, Web, Console). Links to the platform master entity.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'The A/B test variant or experiment group the player was assigned to at the time of the ad impression. Used for ad placement optimization experiments.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'The format or type of ad that was displayed. Defines the user experience and engagement model for the ad impression.']
  ad_network varchar [note: 'The advertising network or mediation platform that served the ad impression. Identifies the source of the ad inventory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: admob|unity_ads|ironsource|applovin|vungle|chartboost|facebook_audience_network|mopub|adcolony|tapjoy|direct — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ad_size varchar [note: 'The dimensions of the ad creative in pixels (e.g., 320x50, 728x90, fullscreen). Used for creative optimization and placement analysis.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the ad impression occurred. Used for geographic revenue analysis and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ad impression record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  days_since_install varchar [note: 'The number of days between the player\'s first app install and this ad impression. Used for cohort analysis and retention modeling.']
  ecpm_revenue decimal(18,6) [note: 'The estimated revenue earned from this ad impression based on eCPM rates. Calculated as (revenue / impressions) * 1000. Used for hybrid LTV modeling.']
  exchange_rate_to_usd decimal(18,6) [note: 'The exchange rate used to convert the revenue amount to USD for consolidated reporting. Rate at the time of impression.']
  fill_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the ad request was successfully filled by the ad network. True if filled, False if no fill. Used for fill rate calculations.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ad impression was flagged as potentially fraudulent by fraud detection systems. True if fraud suspected, False otherwise.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The fraud risk score assigned to this ad impression by fraud detection algorithms. Range 0.00 (no risk) to 100.00 (high risk).']
  impression_date date [note: 'The calendar date when the ad impression occurred. Used for daily aggregation and reporting of ad revenue.']
  impression_status varchar [note: 'The lifecycle status of the ad impression indicating whether it was successfully served, viewed, or encountered an error. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: served|viewed|clicked|completed|skipped|error|timeout — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  impression_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the ad impression was served to the player. Business event timestamp for the ad view.']
  is_first_impression boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this was the player\'s first ad impression in the game. True if first impression, False otherwise. Used for FTUE analysis.']
  is_rewarded boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this ad impression was a rewarded ad (player receives in-game reward for watching). True if rewarded, False if non-rewarded.']
  mediation_waterfall_position varchar [note: 'The position in the ad mediation waterfall where this ad network was called. Used for mediation optimization and fill rate analysis.']
  player_level_at_impression varchar [note: 'The player\'s in-game level or progression tier at the time of the ad impression. Used for player segmentation and ad targeting analysis.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'The geographic region or state code where the ad impression occurred. Used for regional ad performance analysis.']
  revenue_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual revenue earned from this ad impression in the reporting currency. The net proceeds after ad network fees.']
  revenue_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the revenue amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Used for multi-currency revenue reporting.']
  revenue_usd decimal(18,6) [note: 'The revenue amount converted to USD using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and ARPDAU calculations.']
  reward_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the reward granted to the player (e.g., 100 coins, 1 extra life). Null if no reward granted.']
  reward_currency_code varchar [note: 'The virtual currency code or item identifier for the reward granted. Links to the virtual currency or item catalog.']
  reward_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the in-game reward was successfully granted to the player after ad completion. True if reward granted, False if not granted.']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'The type of in-game reward granted to the player for watching the rewarded ad. None if non-rewarded ad or reward not granted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: virtual_currency|extra_life|power_up|unlock|time_skip|loot_box|xp_boost|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ad impression record was last updated in the data platform. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  viewability_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the ad impression met viewability standards (e.g., 50% of pixels visible for 1 second). True if viewable, False otherwise.']
  watch_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the ad video that the player watched before closing or completing. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Critical for rewarded video completion tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every ad view or rewarded video completion by a player. Captures player reference, ad placement reference (FK to ad_placement), impression timestamp, ad network, creative ID, watch completion percentage, reward granted flag and amount, eCPM-based revenue earned, and device/session context. The SSOT for ad revenue events — feeds ARPDAU calculations and hybrid LTV modeling alongside IAP revenue.'
}

Table monetization.price_point {
  price_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price point record. Primary key.']
  commercial_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to licensing.commercial_term. Business justification: Price points for licensed IP content must comply with commercial terms governing minimum guarantees, royalty rates, and territory restrictions. Finance and licensing teams link price points to commerc']
  game_title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the game title for which this price point is configured. Nullable for platform-wide standard price points.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Price points are jurisdiction-specific for tax compliance, VAT/sales tax calculation, currency regulations, and regional pricing strategies. Essential for regional pricing configuration, tax reporting']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Price regulation compliance: price points must comply with jurisdiction-specific regulatory obligations (VAT display requirements, loot box price disclosure laws, consumer protection pricing rules). T']
  superseded_price_point_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_point (superseded_price_point_id)']
  title_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to title.title_sku. Business justification: Price points are defined at the SKU level for platform+region pricing (e.g., PS5 US SKU at $69.99). SKU-level price management, regional pricing compliance, and storefront submission workflows all req']
  age_gate_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether age verification is required before displaying or allowing purchase of this price point (True for compliance with COPPA, GDPR, regional laws).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status for the price point before it goes live: draft (being configured), pending_review (awaiting approval), approved (ready for activation), rejected (not approved).']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price point was approved for publication.']
  base_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base price amount in USD, serving as the reference currency for conversion and reporting.']
  bonus_currency_amount varchar [note: 'Additional bonus virtual currency awarded on top of the base amount (used for promotional bundles or first-time purchase incentives).']
  conversion_optimization_variant varchar [note: 'A/B test or conversion optimization variant identifier for this price point, used to track performance of different pricing strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price point record was first created in the system.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base price for promotional price points (e.g., 25.00 for 25% off).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this price point expires or is no longer available. Nullable for indefinite price points.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this price point becomes active and available for purchase.']
  exchange_rate_to_usd decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert local currency to USD at the time of price point creation or last update.']
  is_default_for_tier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price point is the default or recommended option for its tier level across regions (True) or a regional variant (False).']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price point is part of a limited-time promotional campaign (True) or a standard catalog price (False).']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the local price (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP, KRW).']
  local_price_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Price amount in the local currency for the target market or region.']
  minimum_player_age varchar [note: 'Minimum age required to purchase this price point, enforced for regulatory compliance (e.g., 13, 16, 18).']
  net_proceeds_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the transaction that the publisher receives after platform fees (e.g., 70.00 for 70%).']
  original_price_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Original non-discounted price in USD, used for displaying savings to players during promotions.']
  parental_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental consent is required for minors to purchase this price point (True for COPPA compliance in US, similar regulations in other jurisdictions).']
  platform_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the transaction that the platform (Apple, Google, Steam, etc.) retains as commission (e.g., 30.00 for 30%).']
  price_display_format varchar [note: 'Localized format string for displaying the price to players (e.g., $0.99, €0,99, ¥120), respecting regional conventions.']
  price_point_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the price point, used for external reference and integration with platform storefronts.']
  price_point_description varchar [note: 'Internal notes or description of the price point, including business rationale, target segment, or special conditions.']
  price_point_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the price point shown to players in storefronts.']
  price_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price point: active (live and available), inactive (not currently offered), pending (awaiting platform approval), deprecated (phased out), suspended (temporarily disabled).']
  price_point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price point: standard (regular catalog price), promotional (temporary discount), dynamic (algorithmically adjusted), regional (geo-specific), premium (high-value tier), introductory (first-time buyer).']
  price_rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding convention applied to the local price (e.g., nearest_99 for psychological pricing ending in .99, platform_standard for platform-mandated rounding).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country or region code where this price point applies (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, KOR, EU).']
  tax_inclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price includes applicable taxes (True) or taxes are added at checkout (False).']
  tier_label varchar [note: 'Platform-specific tier label or identifier (e.g., Apple Tier 1, Google Play Tier A).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric tier level representing the price point position in the platform pricing ladder (e.g., 1=lowest, 100=highest).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price point record was last modified.']
  vat_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable VAT or sales tax rate percentage for this price point in the target region (e.g., 20.00 for 20% VAT in UK).']
  virtual_currency_equivalent varchar [note: 'Amount of virtual currency (e.g., coins, gems, credits) that this price point grants to the player upon purchase.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of standardized IAP price points across platforms and currencies, capturing platform-specific pricing tiers, currency conversion rules, and regional pricing strategies.'
}

Table monetization.ad_unit {
  ad_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ad_unit']
  ab_experiment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to analytics.ab_experiment.   parent_ad_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_unit (parent_ad_unit_id)']

  sdk_integration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to platform.sdk_integration. Business justification: Ad units are registered and configured within specific platform SDK integrations. Ad network mediation, floor price enforcement, and platform certification compliance all require knowing which sdk_int']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Media format of the advertisement content served through this ad unit.']
  ad_unit_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the ad unit, used for ad server integration and campaign targeting.']
  ad_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad unit determining whether it can serve advertisements.']
  ad_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ad unit format determining how the advertisement is displayed to the player.']
  advertiser_exclusions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of advertiser identifiers that are blocked from serving ads through this unit due to competitive or content conflicts.']
  auto_refresh_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad unit automatically refreshes to serve new ads at the defined refresh rate interval.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this ad unit becomes active and eligible to serve advertisements.']
  floor_price_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum cost per mille (CPM) bid price in USD required for an ad to be served through this unit, protecting revenue quality.']

  mediation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this ad unit within the mediation waterfall, determining the order in which ad networks are queried.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ad unit record was last modified, tracking configuration changes and optimization updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, optimization insights, or special instructions related to this ad unit.']
  placement_location varchar [note: 'In-game location or screen where the ad unit is displayed, critical for user experience and monetization optimization.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Gaming platform where this ad unit is available for serving advertisements.']

  reward_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantity of the reward granted to players for completing a rewarded ad view.']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'Type of in-game reward granted to players upon successful ad completion for rewarded ad units.']
  rewarded_ad_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ad unit provides in-game rewards (virtual currency, extra lives, power-ups) to players who complete the ad view.']
  skip_offset_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds that must elapse before the skip button becomes available to the player for skippable ads.']
  skippable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether players can skip the ad before completion, impacting user experience and advertiser value.']
  target_audience_age_max varchar [note: 'Maximum age of the intended audience for this ad unit, enabling demographic targeting optimization.']
  target_audience_age_min varchar [note: 'Minimum age of the intended audience for this ad unit, ensuring compliance with age-appropriate advertising regulations.']
  test_mode_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ad unit is operating in test mode, serving test ads for quality assurance without generating revenue.']
  video_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Standard duration in seconds for video ads served through this unit, applicable to video and rewarded video ad types.']
  width_pixels varchar [note: 'Display width of the ad unit in pixels, defining the horizontal dimension of the ad creative space.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ad_unit. Referenced by ad_unit_id.'
}

  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state code where the transaction originated, used for tax and regulatory compliance.']
  storefront_location varchar [note: 'Specific in-game location or menu where the storefront item was displayed and purchased (e.g., main menu, post-match screen, battle pass tab).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the transaction, including sales tax, VAT, or GST as applicable by jurisdiction.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique transaction number displayed to players and used in customer service interactions.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the storefront transaction in the payment and fulfillment workflow.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the player initiated the storefront purchase transaction.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of monetization transaction: in-app purchase (IAP), microtransaction (MTX), downloadable content (DLC), battle pass, subscription, virtual currency purchase, loot box, or season pass.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this transaction record was last modified in the system.']
  virtual_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of virtual currency (e.g., V-Bucks, Apex Coins) granted to the player as part of this transaction.']
  virtual_currency_type varchar [note: 'Name or code of the virtual currency type awarded in this transaction.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for storefront_transaction. Referenced by storefront_transaction_id.'
}

  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or process that last modified this storefront item record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this storefront item record was last updated.']

  player_level_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum player level required to view or purchase this storefront item (null if no level gate).']
  purchase_limit_per_player varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single player can purchase this item (null for unlimited purchases).']
  region_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions or markets where this item is available, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region codes.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence number for controlling the presentation order of items in the storefront interface.']
  stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Total available inventory quantity for limited-edition items (null for unlimited digital goods).']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of searchable tags or keywords for storefront filtering and discovery (e.g., new, trending, exclusive, limited).']
  total_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative revenue generated from all sales of this storefront item in the base currency.']
  total_sales_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times this storefront item has been purchased across all players and platforms.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this storefront item record, incremented with each modification for optimistic locking and change tracking.']
  video_preview_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the video preview or trailer showcasing the storefront item (null if no video available).']
  virtual_currency_price varchar [note: 'Price of the storefront item in the game\'s virtual currency (e.g., V-Bucks, Riot Points, Apex Coins) if purchasable with in-game currency.']
  virtual_currency_type varchar [note: 'Name or code of the virtual currency used for pricing (e.g., gold, gems, premium_coins) if applicable.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user or process that created this storefront item record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for storefront_item. Referenced by storefront_item_id.'

Table monetization.plan_content_inclusion {
  plan_content_inclusion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the plan content inclusion association record']
  dlc_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the DLC content that is included in the subscription plan library']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the subscription plan that includes this DLC content in its library']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access granted to subscribers for this DLC content (FULL = complete access, TRIAL = limited time trial, PREVIEW = demo mode, TIMED = time-limited sessions). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  content_unlock_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position in which this DLC content unlocks for subscribers (e.g., episodic content released over subscription tenure). Null if all content available immediately. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plan content inclusion record was first created in the system. Used for audit and change tracking.']
  exclusion_date date [note: 'Date when this DLC content will be or was removed from the subscription plan library. Null if content is permanent. Used for content rotation management and subscriber notifications.']
  included_since_date date [note: 'Date when this DLC content was added to the subscription plan library. Used for content lifecycle tracking and subscriber communication. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  inclusion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this content inclusion (ACTIVE = currently available, SCHEDULED = future inclusion, EXPIRED = removed from library, SUSPENDED = temporarily unavailable). Used for access enforcement and content catalog display.']
  is_exclusive_to_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DLC content is exclusive to this subscription plan tier (not available in lower tiers or standalone purchase). Used for marketing differentiation and access control. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plan content inclusion record was last modified. Used for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the inclusion of DLC content within subscription plan libraries. It captures which DLC entitlements are available to subscribers of each plan, when content enters or exits the library, access restrictions, and exclusivity arrangements. Each record links one subscription_plan to one DLC content offering with attributes that govern subscriber access rights and content lifecycle within the subscription service.. Existence Justification: In gaming subscription services (Xbox Game Pass, PlayStation Plus, EA Play model), subscription plans include curated libraries of DLC content, where each plan tier includes multiple DLC items and each DLC can be included across multiple subscription tiers with different access levels. The business actively manages content library composition, tracking when content enters/exits plans, enforcing tier-specific access rules, and managing exclusivity arrangements. This is an operational M:N relationship representing subscription content library management.'
}

  storefront_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the digital distribution storefront. Primary key.']
  ad_network_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.ad_network.
